Empires & Puzzles Dizzy Review: The Anti-Holy Rockstar

Meet Dizzy, the moody musician of the Winter Family. With a guitar made of bone and a hatred for all things Holy, she brings a unique mechanic to the 4-star arena. But with stats from yesteryear, can this gothic elf still rock the boat, or is she just singing the blues?
Dizzy, The Gothic Elf: Sad Songs and Yellow Reflects

Meet Dizzy, the moody, gothic musician of the Winter Family who looks like she just left a My Chemical Romance concert to join Santa's workshop—reluctantly. Released during the 2023 Santa's Challenge, she brings a very specific set of skills to the table: making Holy heroes regret their existence. As an Epic (4-star) Dark Wizard, she functions as a niche counter-hero, channeling the spirit of the legendary Ursena but scaling it down for the 4-star arena. She doesn't have a costume yet, so she's stuck wearing her original leather-and-spikes ensemble.

Dizzy’s design is a hilarious contrast to the cheerful holiday spirit of the Winter Family. While Buddy and Rudolph are busy being festive, Dizzy is rocking corpse paint, a purple beanie, and a "don't talk to me" attitude. She wields a lute that looks suspiciously like a bone, ready to strum a melody of despair. The art perfectly captures the "it's not a phase, mom" energy, standing out distinctly against the snowy backdrops and jolly elves of the event. It's refreshing to see an elf who hates Christmas as much as the Grinch, but with better fashion sense.

Let's be real: Dizzy's stats are starting to gather dust. With a base Power of 687, Attack of 675, and Defense of 684, she is significantly behind the curve compared to modern 4-stars like the Construct family or the latest Toon costumes, which regularly push 800+ Attack and 1500+ HP. While her stats were respectable in late 2023, in the current meta, she is fragile. She needs emblems and a Limit Break just to survive a sneeze from a modern sniper.
"Gloomy Tune" is a fascinating mix of damage, support, and niche countering.
250% to three targets is "okay" but not game-changing. It softens the enemy, but don't expect it to wipe the board.
The Elf Minions are standard for the family (10% HP/Attack). They add a small layer of meat shield, which Dizzy desperately needs given her aging stats.
This is the main event. Reflecting

from Holy Special Skills is incredibly powerful—but only against Yellows. If the enemy team has no Holy hitters, this part of the skill is useless. However, against a Li Xiu, Hu Tao, or a Joon, Dizzy becomes a wall of pain.
Average (10 tiles). With a Lv. 23 Mana Troop, you can drop this to 9 tiles.
At ~675 Attack, a 250% hit deals roughly 1687 raw damage (before defense). Spread across three targets, it's decent chip damage but lacks knockout power.
~125 HP per minion (base stats). It's a tiny speed bump for modern heroes.
Potentially infinite. If a Holy sniper hits her for 1000 damage, they take 1000 damage. This scales with the enemy's power, which is Dizzy's saving grace against power creep.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Winter Family is all about minions, and Dizzy fits right in.

The bonus boosts minion stats (Health and Attack). It’s nice if you run her with other Winter heroes (like Penguin or Mother North), making the minions slightly less paper-thin. In a vacuum, it's a minor perk.
Dizzy does not have any innate passives, which is another sign of her age. Modern heroes usually come with two or three passives; Dizzy just comes with her guitar.

At the start of battle, her attack is increased by 20% for 6 turns. This helps her initial hit and minion damage, but honestly, a defensive Aether Power like Damage Reduction or Dodge would have been better to keep her alive long enough to cast her reflect.
Upon release, Dizzy was hailed as the "4-star Ursena," a strategic tool for 4-star tournaments to shut down Yellow tanks and flanks.
Her stats have fallen off a cliff. Modern 4-stars have 20-30% higher stats. However, her
—Elemental Reflect—is timeless. A reflected hit from a 2026 Holy hero hurts just as much as one from 2023.

She is not a general-purpose hero. You don't bring her to a nature fight. But in specific scenarios—specifically 4-star tournaments or wars against Holy-heavy teams—she remains a viable counter-pick, provided you protect her.
This hero does not currently have a costume.
Dizzy's primary role is to survive long enough to cast her Reflect. If she dies before firing, she's useless. Therefore, we prioritize bulk.
Maximizes survivability, ensuring she lives to cast Gloomy Tune.
Increases damage of the hit-3 and minion pokes.
She will likely die before charging her mana.
path is recommended. Her value is in the Reflect, not the 250% slap. Keep her alive.
Completely neutralizes Yellow damage dealers and status effect casters (like Li Xiu's mana cut or blindness).
The Reflect damage scales with the enemy's strength. The stronger they are, the harder they hit themselves.
Provides a small buffer of HP for herself and neighbors.
Extremely low base stats compared to modern heroes make her squishy.
The Reflect only works against Holy Special Skills. Useless against tile damage or other elements.
10 tiles can be too slow in today's Fast/Very Fast meta, especially to set up a defensive buff.
Reflects only for Caster and Nearby (3 heroes), not the whole team.
In Rush, she can fire quickly to set up the wall. In Bloody Battle, minions provide pseudo-healing.
As a counter-pick against teams using heavy Yellow flanks or tanks.
Useful against Yellow bosses who cast nasty ailments.
Dizzy shines when she is the answer to a specific question: "How do I stop that Yellow hero?"
Kiril keeps her alive, Tiburtus drops defense, and Dizzy reflects. Place Dizzy where the Yellow enemy is most likely to hit (usually flank or tank position).
Stack the Winter Family bonus to make beefier minions.
Her Reflect is a buff. Sabina or Melendor can wipe it away instantly.
Rangers or heroes with "Bypass Defensive Buffs" can ignore her Reflect. Be very careful against modern Rangers.
Heroes like Grimble or Alucard eat her minions for breakfast.

If you pull Dizzy, lock her. She is a tool you will need eventually. Don't prioritize her over a solid healer or a general sniper, but once you have your basics, level her up for Tournaments. She is a "get out of jail free" card against difficult Yellow bosses.

She's likely a bench-warmer for you outside of very specific 4-star tournaments. Unless you are struggling with Yellow titans (where the reflect damage is capped anyway) or specific 4-star Raid challenges, she isn't worth a Limit Break 2 (Alpha Aether). Her stats are just too low to compete in legendary wars.
The community immediately latched onto her design. "Gothic really depressed looking," said one player, while another noted she looks like she listens to "Gloomy Sunday" on repeat.
Players instantly recognized her skill as a mini-Ursena. "Elemental reflect is a very strong ability... definitely worth maxing unless you have multiple Ursenas."
"Is she too niche?" was a common question. The consensus? Yes, but in that niche, she is a queen. "I cannot think of any use except raids/tourney offence team when enemies have lots of yellow."

Dizzy is the definition of a "silver bullet." In most fights, she's an under-statted 4-star with average speed. But when a Holy hero loads their gun, Dizzy turns it right back around on them. While her stats are singing the blues, her mechanic remains heavy metal. Keep her for those specific tournaments, but don't expect her to lead your main defense team.
A situational rockstar. Keep one copy, play it loud when the Yellows come to town.
